title: test-suite
description: The verifier for capability cells in the app family. A capability advances to `validated` only when its test suite exists, passes, and clears the coverage floor. This rubric must itself be `validated` (deterministic + calibrated) before it gates any capability. The gates are mechanical and run by bin/test-suite-check.py against the capability's test artifact + the suite's machine-readable result; mutation-resistance and oracle-quality are the critic's judgment. The pristine reference is the runner's own exit status — minted from an external process, never the worker's assertion.
maturity_precondition: This rubric cell must be validated (deterministic on fixed input; calibrated against the exemplar set) before it gates any capability.

calibration.critic_model: an independent critic tier, NOT the code-authoring agent
recalibration_triggers: a false-pass observed in the ledger (green suite, shipped defect)
calibration.note: Few-shot scored suites spanning the scale: a strong suite (real assertions, above-floor coverage, failure-mode tests), a no-test capability (asserting done with zero tests), a vacuous suite (tests that assert nothing / always pass), and a below-floor near-miss. An uncalibrated judge drifts generous toward model-authored tests that exercise nothing.

pristine_reference.enforced_by: validate.py mints the signal from the EXTERNAL runner's exit status (gate-verifier); the worker never hand-asserts pass
pristine_reference.note: The test runner is an external process the worker does not control: validate.py runs it and reads its exit status. A worker can write tests but it cannot make a failing run report success — the verdict comes from the runner, not the worker's opinion. The coverage report is read from the runner's machine-readable output, not self-reported.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: The capability ships a test artifact with at least one real test case (a discoverable, non-empty suite that asserts something). A capability claimed 'validated' with zero tests fails here.
dimensions.fail_meaning: No tests means 'validated' is the worker's opinion, not a measured signal.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: The suite RUNS GREEN: the runner's recorded result reports zero failures and zero errors. The verdict is the external runner's exit status (minted by validate.py), never the worker's self-report.
dimensions.mechanized_by: bin/test-suite-check.py (tests-pass, read from the runner's machine-readable result)
dimensions.fail_meaning: A red or un-run suite is not a passing capability; advancing on it ships a known-broken cell.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: Measured line/branch coverage clears the declared floor (default 70%, overridable per cell). The number is read from the runner's coverage report, not self-reported. This is a higher-order check: a property of the suite's reach over the code, not just that it ran.
dimensions.fail_meaning: A green suite that exercises 10% of the code is green theatre; the untested 90% is unverified.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Do the tests assert MEANINGFUL behaviour (real oracles tied to the spec's acceptance criteria), or do they assert trivia / tautologies that pass regardless of correctness? Would a plausible injected defect actually turn the suite red?
dimensions.fail_meaning: Coverage without oracles is exercised-but-unchecked code; the suite is green for the wrong reason.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Beyond line coverage: would the suite CATCH a realistic mutation (a flipped comparison, a dropped branch, an off-by-one)? Are edge cases and the spec's failure modes tested, not just the happy path?
dimensions.fail_meaning: A suite that survives mutation tests the absence of crashes, not the presence of correctness.

scoring.gates: ALL gate dimensions must pass; any gate failure returns the capability to regenerating regardless of review score. bin/test-suite-check.py runs the gate dimensions mechanically and exits 0 iff all pass.
scoring.review: Weighted review score must clear each dimension threshold AND an aggregate of 80/100.
scoring.signal: On pass, the CRITIC (not the code-authoring agent) writes signals/{capability-cell}/{ts}--test-suite.json via the validation path. signals/ is deny-on-write to the worker (gate-signal). The gate verdict rests on the external runner's exit status, not the worker's claim.
